question_title: How many Artemis Accords signatories will NASA list on its Artemis Accords page before April 30, 2026?
summary: As of March 16, 2026, NASA's Artemis Accords page lists exactly 61 signatories (most recent: Oman on January 26, 2026). The question asks for the count as of April 30, 2026 — a 45-day window. There has been a 7-week gap since the last signing, suggesting some rate deceleration from the ~0.75/month pace seen in 2025. However, the Artemis II launch scheduled for April 1, 2026 is a major catalyst, as NASA has historically coordinated new signatory announcements around milestones (e.g., 3 countries at the 5th anniversary in Oct 2025). Monte Carlo modeling with a blended rate + catalyst approach yields fairly balanced probabilities across all three buckets, with genuine uncertainty about whether any new signatories will join in the window.

factors.description: Current count is exactly 61 signatories, confirmed by NASA page and Wikipedia. If nothing changes, question resolves ≤61.
7-week gap since last signing (Jan 26 to Mar 16) with no new announcements, suggesting the signing rate may be slowing from the 2025 pace of ~0.75/month

Artemis II launch scheduled for April 1, 2026 — NASA historically coordinates new signatory announcements around major Artemis events (e.g., 3 countries at the 5th anniversary in Oct 2025). This is the biggest catalyst for new signings in the window.
Historical signing rate of ~0.75/month in 2025. Over 1.5 months this gives ~1.1 expected new signatories, with the median outcome near exactly 62.
2024 had 19 new signatories but 2025 only ~9 — the rate is decelerating as the pool of willing/ready signatories shrinks (already 61 of ~193 UN members, most aligned nations already signed)
45 days is a moderate window. Even at a reduced rate of 0.5/month, Monte Carlo shows ~50% probability of at least 1 new signatory; at 0.75/month, ~65%
Trump administration maintains Artemis as a presidential priority, and NASA Administrator Isaacman is actively promoting the program, supporting continued outreach for new signatories

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary:**

| Option | Probability |
|--------|------------|
| ≤61 | **38%** |
| 62 | **28%** |
| >62 | **34%** |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Current state:** NASA's page lists exactly 61 signatories (Oman was #61, signed Jan 26, 2026). No new signatories in the 7 weeks since.
- **Status quo favors ≤61:** If no country signs before April 30, the count stays at 61. The 7-week gap and decelerating rate (19 new in 2024, ~9 in 2025) suggest some slowing.
- **Artemis II catalyst:** The first crewed lunar mission in 50+ years launches April 1 — NASA has historically coordinated signatory announcements around major milestones (e.g., 3 countries at the Oct 2025 anniversary). This meaningfully raises the odds of new signings.
- **Balanced uncertainty:** Monte Carlo modeling with a ~0.5-0.6/month base rate + Artemis II catalyst produces roughly equal probability across all three buckets, reflecting genuine uncertainty about whether 0, 1, or 2+ countries will join in the 45-day window.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) How many Artemis Accords signatories will NASA list on its Artemis Accords page before April 30, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42637)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the number of signatories listed on NASA’s Artemis Accords page at https://www.nasa.gov/artemis-accords/ as of  April 30, 2026.
fine_print: Use the count implied by the signatories listed on the official NASA page. If NASA changes the layout, count the countries shown as current signatories on that page. Candidate Sources: NASA's main Artemis site at https://www.nasa.gov/artemis/ ; the Artemis Accords page itself.
